# Attribution — tufte-viz

**Author:** [@aparente](https://github.com/aparente)

**Description:** Edward Tufte data visualization principles applied as a Claude Code skill.

## License note

The original gist does not include an explicit LICENSE file. As a public gist on GitHub, it is publicly viewable. This directory is a mirror copy with full attribution to the original author. If you are the author and would like the license clarified or this copy removed, please open an issue at https://github.com/MauroProto/mis-skills/issues.

## Structure changes from the gist

The original gist uses `__` (double underscore) in filenames as a workaround for the fact that gists don't support subdirectories. This copy restores the intended hierarchy:

```
gist filename                            →  this directory
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
references__tufte-principles.md          →  references/tufte-principles.md
references__analytical-design.md         →  references/analytical-design.md
demo__giss-temperature.html              →  demos/giss-temperature.html
demo__kyoto-sakura.html                  →  demos/kyoto-sakura.html
demo__sunspot-butterfly.html             →  demos/sunspot-butterfly.html
demo__sunspot-pretty.html                →  demos/sunspot-pretty.html
```

`SKILL.md` is unchanged.

## What this skill teaches

Edward Tufte's principles from his four core books:
- *The Visual Display of Quantitative Information* (1983)
- *Envisioning Information* (1990)
- *Visual Explanations* (1997)
- *Beautiful Evidence* (2006)

Including: data-ink ratio, lie factor, chartjunk elimination, small multiples, sparklines, layering & separation, micro/macro design, range-frames, the eraser test, the collision test.

Applies to any task involving charts, dashboards, data tables, analytics displays, or visual reports.
